description: Letter from Natan ha-Kohen ha-Mumḥe b. Mevorakh (a.k.a. Wahb b. Barakāt), in Ashqelon, to the parnas ʿEli b. Ḥayyim ha-Kohen (a.k.a. Abū l-Ḥasan ʿAllūn b. Yaʿīsh), in Fustat. In Hebrew, with the address in Arabic. Gil describes this as a draft but does not explain why he thinks so. He also notes that this is not the handwriting of the well-known Natan b. Mevorakh ha-Kohen who sent letters from Ashqelon during the period ca. 1090–1140 CE. He suggests that the sender of this letter is the grandfather of the well-known Natan b. Mevorakh and that this letter was likely written around 1075 CE (he understands the vague reference to distressing times in ll. 8–9 to be a reference to the Seljuks in Palestine).
description_he: מכתב מנתן הכהן המומחה בן מבורך מאשקלון לאלי בן חיים הכהן בפוסטאט, בערך 1075.
